pattern: Fast exponentiation halves the exponent each step, squaring the base, so a power that would take n multiplications needs only about log n.
hints: Raising to an even power is the square of raising to half that power.
A negative exponent is just the reciprocal of the positive-exponent result.

Reduce the exponent toward zero by squaring the base and halving the exponent, multiplying the running result by the base whenever the current exponent bit is odd; invert at the end if the exponent was negative.

edge_inputs.description: fractional base with a small exponent
negative base with an odd exponent stays negative
negative base with an even exponent turns positive
extreme negative exponent must not overflow when negated
base of one stays one under any exponent

stress.description: random base and modest exponent kept within representable bounds
stress.note: exponent kept small so the magnitude stays well within double precision
